title: Scaffold rvagent-engine crate implementing TaskRunner
objective: Create meta-ruvector/crates/rvAgent/rvagent-engine as a workspace member with a stub `impl rvagent_a2a::TaskRunner`, compiling clippy-clean, so the Router/budget/recursion-guard machinery composes around it.
acceptance_criteria: crate is a member of the rvAgent workspace family
cargo check -p rvagent-engine passes
cargo clippy -p rvagent-engine --all-targets -- -D warnings is clean
provides a stub impl of rvagent_a2a::TaskRunner (run + cancel)
completion_gate: clippy-clean workspace member with TaskRunner stub
rollback_plan: git rm the new crate dir; revert the workspace Cargo.toml member addition
notes: No parallel task type — implement the EXISTING TaskRunner trait (rvagent-a2a/src/executor.rs:31). Stub only; TEASTASK-011 de-stubs run(). path_scope rooted at the lifeos workspace root (/home/flexnetos/lifeos).

title: WorkOrder <-> TaskSpec anti-corruption adapter (seam S3)
objective: Map handoff.task.v1 WorkOrder <-> rvagent-a2a TaskSpec/Task, round-tripping while preserving the blake3 IntentLock; TaskSpec.skill is the routing key.
acceptance_criteria: WorkOrder -> TaskSpec -> WorkOrder round-trips losslessly
IntentLock (blake3: hashes) preserved across the round-trip
unit tests cover the mapping incl. status enum and priority
rollback_plan: revert the adapter module; TEASTASK-001 stub remains
notes: Anti-corruption layer per DOMAIN_MODEL S3. The round-trip is between handoff's origin WorkOrder struct (work-order/src/lib.rs:57) and rvagent-engine's schemars-generated WorkOrder type (from TEASTASK-003); two Rust types coexist until convergence (DOMAIN_MODEL 9.6).

title: Canonical schemas -> Rust types via schemars (heal 9.1/9.2)
objective: Generate Rust types from teas/schemas/task_graph.schema.json and proof_record.schema.json (schemars single source of truth), replacing the divergent 40-col/17-col CSV and three proof shapes.
acceptance_criteria: WorkOrder and ProofRecord Rust types derive schemars::JsonSchema
emitted schema is byte-equal to the checked-in canonical schemas (drift gate)
CSV projection is generated from the type, not hand-maintained
rollback_plan: revert type module; canonical JSON schemas remain the contract
notes: Heals DOMAIN_MODEL 9.1 (one task-graph schema) and the SCHEMA half of 9.2 (proof types). The canonical schemas (lifeos/planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/teas/schemas) are a READ-ONLY input to the drift gate — not in path_scope, which is the write scope. The S5 runtime reconcile (batch proof_ledger.jsonl <-> handoff redb ledger) is deferred post-v1 (DOMAIN_MODEL S5).

title: Implement rvagent-engine::run to actually execute a WorkOrder (de-stub the runner)
objective: Replace the TaskRunner stub with a real run() that dispatches the WorkOrder via rvagent-backends (local_shell/sandbox) / rvagent-core, executes the verification_command, and captures pass/fail — so completion reflects real execution, never an echo (InMemoryRunner at executor.rs:106).
acceptance_criteria: rvagent-engine::run executes the WorkOrder's work (not an echo) via rvagent-backends
the verification_command runs and its pass/fail is captured as the run verdict
a failing verification yields status failed with failure_reason, never Done
an integration test drives a trivial WorkOrder to a real Done
completion_gate: real execution + verdict capture test green
rollback_plan: revert run() body to the stub; TEASTASK-001 scaffold remains
notes: Closes the review gap: TEASTASK-007 must NOT ride on the InMemoryRunner echo. Reuse rvagent-backends local_shell.rs/sandbox.rs; do not build a new executor.

title: ProofRecord writer to the handoff witnessed ledger (seam S4)
objective: rvagent-engine writes a teas.proof_record.v1 ProofRecord into handoff's redb witnessed ledger (blake3 witness chain) on task completion; proof status overrides card status.
acceptance_criteria: a completed run appends a valid ProofRecord to the ledger
witness chain verifies after append (verify_witness_chain)
replay yields the correct latest status
rollback_plan: ledger is append-only; revert the writer code, leave prior events intact
notes: Reality plane = handoff ledger (DOMAIN_MODEL S4). Depends on TEASTASK-011 so the proof records a REAL run verdict, not an echo.

title: gitkb ready -> handoff claim selection wire (seam S2)
objective: Adapter that reads `git-kb ready --json` (scored selection), resolves the slug to a WorkOrder, and issues an atomic `hf claim` lease so exactly one agent owns it.
acceptance_criteria: given git-kb ready output, the top task maps to a WorkOrder id
hf claim acquires a single-holder lease (CAS)
conflict on an already-claimed task is refused, ledger untouched
rollback_plan: revert selection adapter; gitkb + hf remain independently usable
notes: TEAS conforms to gitkb ranking (Conformist). Requires HF_LEASE_HOLDER for multi-agent exclusion.

title: prompt_hub ExecutionPlan -> WorkOrder emitter (seam S1, greenfield)
objective: New prompt_hub module that maps Intent+ExecutionPlan.steps to handoff.task.v1 WorkOrder rows (filling owner_lane, path_scope, verification_command, proof_uri, proof_required), closing the missing front-door wire.
acceptance_criteria: an ExecutionPlan emits >=1 WorkOrder validating against task_graph.schema.json
dependencies derive from ExecutionStep.dependencies (toposort preserved)
emitted WorkOrders carry a computed IntentLock
rollback_plan: revert the taskgraph module; prompt_hub vibe pipeline unaffected
notes: The biggest greenfield seam (zero refs today). DOMAIN_MODEL S1.

title: End-to-end proof: one WorkOrder through the unified loop (v1 success gate)
objective: Drive ONE WorkOrder end-to-end: prompt_hub intent -> gitkb ready -> handoff claim -> rvagent-engine run (real execution) -> witnessed proof on the ledger -> next-action, every gate enforced, no paper completion. This is NORTH_STAR §7 v1.
acceptance_criteria: one WorkOrder transitions Backlog -> ... -> Done via a REAL run (TEASTASK-011), not an echo
a ProofRecord for it verifies on the handoff ledger (witness chain)
gate refusals are enforced by their real owners: claim/lease by handoff-lease; network/drift/approval by handoff's fail-closed gate engine (handoff-drift/handoff-policy), not assumed
no Done without proof (ledger authority holds)
completion_gate: end-to-end WorkOrder Done with verified proof
rollback_plan: test-only milestone; no production mutation; revert test harness if needed
notes: The milestone that declares the engine real. Transitively gated on TEASTASK-011 (real runner) via TEASTASK-004. Human approval = confirm the proof before declaring v1.

title: Trajectory capture into sona ReasoningBank (seam S6 learning)
objective: rvagent-engine records each execution as a sona Trajectory feeding the ReasoningBank, so every cycle improves future selection/execution (capability-gain law).
acceptance_criteria: a completed run appends a Trajectory to the ReasoningBank
reward/verdict is recorded per step
recall returns the trajectory by semantic query
rollback_plan: revert the sona wire; ReasoningBank untouched
notes: Reuse rvagent-middleware sona pipeline (rvagent-middleware/src/sona.rs); do not rebuild.

title: Heal the dual-workspace shadow (contradiction 9.4)
objective: Collapse the overlapping src/Cargo.toml (FlexNetOS/meta v0.2.25) vs src/meta/Cargo.toml (gitkb/meta v0.2.22) so meta's agent/ crate builds in-tree; superset merge, no capability loss.
acceptance_criteria: exactly one workspace owns each crate; no 'believes it's in a workspace' error
meta agent/ crate builds in-tree
no crate or capability dropped (superset)
completion_gate: one-workspace resolution, agent/ builds, superset preserved
rollback_plan: restore both Cargo.toml files from git; no crates deleted
notes: Structural cross-repo change — human approval required (touches workspace topology). path_scope rooted at the lifeos workspace root: src/Cargo.toml (FlexNetOS/meta) and src/meta/Cargo.toml (gitkb/meta).

title: Expose rvagent-engine task ops as MCP tools (rvagent-mcp registry)
objective: Surface the unified engine's task operations (submit/claim/run/proof/status) as MCP tools via the existing rvagent-mcp registry, so Claude Code / other hosts drive TEAS over MCP.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tools/list exposes the engine's task operations
tools/call submit->claim->run->proof works over stdio
parity with the hf-mcp surface for the shared operations
rollback_plan: revert the MCP tool registrations; core engine unaffected
notes: Reuse rvagent-mcp::registry; mirror hf-mcp's 38-tool surface for shared ops.
